answer: When Andrew realises that the young helmsman steering the ship was Christ, he speaks directly to God (paragraph [p-775061] of cynewulf-andreas-07b573, chapter 1): 'Now I, Lord God, have understood / that you on the sea-path were not far away, / Glory of kings, when I climbed in the keel, / though I on the wave-journey, Prince of angels, / Giver of grace to souls, could not perceive you. / Be merciful to me now, mighty Maker, / joyful, bright King! I on the brine-stream spoke / a host of words; I know now after / who with honors in that wooden boat / ferried me over the floods.' When Christ then appears before him in person (paragraph [p-ee08ea]), Andrew falls to earth and adds a penitent coda: 'How did I do this, Wielder of men, sinful against myself, Savior of souls, that I, you so good, could not perceive on the path of the waves, where I spoke more of my words before my Maker than I should have?'

tool_calls.name: read_chapter
tool_calls.result_summary: Full 83-paragraph translation retrieved; found recognition scene in [p-775061] and [p-ee08ea]
